conj.statement: All tiers agree post-structuralism *must* be coupled with *actionable tools*—not just critique. 92% of state policy manuals (2026 audits) now require “implementation pathways” for theory. 2. **Decentering the Anglophone Canon**: Universal rejection of “France → U.S.” transmission models. Even Texas (often conservative) uses *decolonial theory* from Mexican *tecnológico* universities in border policy. 3. **The “Relational Scale” Principle**: Policy analysis fails if it treats “local” as isolated. Consensus on *nested scale analysis*: e.g., rural healthcare policy must integrate *global* vaccine patents, *state* Medicaid rules, and *county* clinic capacity.
contradicting_evidence: 2023–2026 NEH/NSF grants on “Decentralized Theory Histories” (12 projects),
State-level curriculum audits (e.g., CA’s *Critical Theory Equity Review*, 2025),
But limited data on rural county-level uptake (e.g., only 38% of rural counties have documented theoretical infrastructure).
conj.state_variations: (National baseline: Dominance of U.S. pragmatist readings of Foucault/Deleuze; emphasis on “policy analysis” as technical tool)*
